picks.reason: Max Meyer’s 2.81 ERA, 9.9 K/9 and solid underlying metrics give MIA the clearer pitching edge against Alvarez’s tiny 20-IP sample. Washington’s bullpen is compromised after Miles Mikolas threw 92 pitches yesterday while MIA’s closer is fully available. Near-even money does not reflect this late-inning advantage in good weather.

TB’s bullpen is heavily taxed after multiple high-leverage arms worked 13-40 pitches yesterday while DET has only one taxed arm and a fresh closer. Melton’s small-sample 1.74 ERA is fragile but Nick Martinez’s 4.15 xERA signals regression. TB’s poor 2-8 recent form adds to the value on the plus-money road side.

MIN’s closer is taxed on back-to-back days and their last-10 record is 3-7. Bradley’s surface stats are good but CHW’s 7-3 recent form, positive run differential and fresher bullpen create a spot where the underdog price is mispriced. xERA gap between Fedde and Bradley is modest.

Kirby’s 3.45 xERA edges Peralta and SEA owns a strong +30 run differential plus an 8-2 last-10 mark at home. NYM bullpen is badly taxed (one arm at 83 pitches yesterday) while SEA’s relief is mostly fresh. Implied probability lags the true edge.

Cristopher Sánchez’s 1.46 ERA / 2.91 xERA / 10.7 K/9 is ace level against Buehler’s 4.53 ERA and 4.72 xERA. The total collapsing from 7.5 to 5.5 confirms the market expects PHI dominance. PHI’s closer is available and the plus-money run line efficiently captures the expected margin.

The line move from +124 to +3300 is too extreme to ignore and implies unlisted news about one of the starters or a key injury. Without that context Tolle’s strong xERA versus Bassitt’s poor numbers cannot be trusted at face value. No actionable edge.

The massive swing from CLE +141 to -394 strongly suggests pitcher availability or injury news not captured in the listed starters. Williams’ high xERA versus Cole’s small-sample 2.00 ERA plus NYY’s +93 run differential cannot be properly weighted. Pass.

Chase Burns (2.05 ERA, 2.89 xERA, 10.4 K/9) is a clear step above Kolek’s small-sample profile. KC owns baseball’s worst run differential and road offense. CIN’s bullpen, while taxed, is still better positioned than KC’s in a park that should play neutral-to-favorable for the home side.

The line movement from +125 to +475 / -556 is dramatic and implies unlisted information on pitching, lineup or injury. Corbin’s 5.28 xERA is poor but ATL’s home dominance cannot be fully trusted at these adjusted prices without the missing context.

MIL leads the league in run differential (+93) and boasts strong home numbers while SF is among the worst teams in both categories. Gasser’s 12-IP sample is too small to trust; Webb has been mediocre. MIL’s bullpen is fresher. Modest edge remains.

Gore and Pallante have nearly identical xERAs (3.98 / 3.87) in similar workloads. Teams are comparable in form and run differential. STL’s taxed closer is a negative but the adjusted total and near-even ML have efficiently priced the matchup.

Sharp line movement the opposite direction of opening numbers plus Rea’s poor 5.14 xERA versus Springs’ 3.87 xERA creates conflicting signals. Both bullpens are moderately taxed and weather is neutral. No clear inefficiency.

Skenes’ 2.28 xERA and 10.3 K/9 is markedly better than Arrighetti’s 4.69 xERA hidden behind a 1.29 ERA. PIT has the superior run differential (+35 vs -37), better recent form and a more rested closer. Market has not fully corrected for the pitching regression.

Ureña’s 2.44 ERA / 3.31 xERA is substantially better than Lorenzen’s 7.22 ERA / 5.89 xERA. Both clubs are bad but LAA is at home with a fresher bullpen. Price is reasonable but still leaves a small positive edge on the home side.

Ohtani’s 0.82 ERA / 2.40 xERA / 10.0 K/9 is elite against Gallen’s 5.16 ERA / 5.21 xERA. LAD’s +127 run differential dwarfs AZ’s. Even with some LAD bullpen tax, the starting pitching gap and offensive superiority support covering -1.5 more often than this price implies.

best_bet.why_this: Sánchez’s elite 1.46 ERA / 2.91 xERA against Buehler’s 4.72 xERA is the clearest single pitching mismatch on the slate, reinforced by the total collapsing to 5.5 and PHI’s fresher closer. It exceeds the edge found in the PIT and LAD games.
